revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kusaka no Hatabi no hime]][[fa:کوساکو نو هاتابی نو هیمه]][[ja:草香幡梭姫皇女]][[zh:幡梭皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Yūryaku|qid=Q357235}}|father={{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Hanzei|qid=Q329769}}; {{ill|Ingyō|qid=Q349258}}; {{ill|Prince Ookusaka|qid=Q11438673}}; {{ill|Prince Suminoe no Nakatsu|qid=Q11381805}}; {{ill|Richū|qid=Q329704}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11618290]] Wikidata name: Princess Kusaka no hatabihime no Himemiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|草香幡梭姫皇女|date=January 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Kusaka no Hatabi no hime |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 457–479 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = after 457 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Kusaka no Hatabi no hime''' (? – after 457) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]].<ref> Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Nakashi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Naniwa no Ono]] | years=457–479}} {{s-end}}

'''Kusaka no Hatabi no hime''' (? – after 457) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]].<ref> Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

'''Kusaka no Hatabi no hime''' (? – after 457) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}.<ref> Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>

She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.
